methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Einride
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Harbinger
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Waabi, a Series C autonomous vehicle software company founded by Raquel Urtasun, operates in the Transportation sector alongside a limited peer universe of seven private companies. No public comps exist in this narrow segment; private peers include Einride, Harbinger, Rivian, The Boring Company, and REGENT Craft, though few operate at identical stage and valuation tier.

Given $1.0B in total capital raised to date, the Stage-Progression method is the primary applicable valuation lens. Applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C-to-next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $1.7B. A Peer-Median approach was not viable; while five Transportation peers exist, insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables (fewer than three datapoints meeting narrow filters) preclude meaningful benchmarking without introducing false precision.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.4B to $2.0B, with a median point of $1.7B. This reflects reasonable upside from deployed capital while acknowledging the stage-specific step-up convention. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct same-stage public or private comps, which limits external validation, and the nascent state of autonomous software valuations where margin profiles and revenue multiples remain unproven at scale. Further refinement would benefit from near-term revenue traction or additional Series C-stage peer data.
